source_file: The corporate social credit system in China and its transnational impact.txt
text:
credit system (SCS) with the majority of the data sets focussing on the commer-
cial and ﬁnancial sectors in order to regulate corporations as well as the ﬁnancial
credit worthiness of individuals. Yet, these are connected to data sets on social
behaviour and the aim to control the political discourse.11 Thus, albeit the
ﬁnancial focus, the SCS has relevance for social and political control.12 In the
envisioned form of the SCS, big data is the desired methodology to establish
a uniﬁed system. The collection and assessment of data, and thus also the assess-
ment of corporate behaviour, is ‘outsourced’ to algorithms.13 Even though it is
not yet clear to what extent automation will play a role,14 it is stated as a desir-
able aim.15 At present most research on the SCS focusses on local pilot projects
and interpretations of policy documents and its envisioned characteristics and
aims. As there is little empirical research on the de facto impact on corporations,
the analysis presented here is a tentative one. Nevertheless, it can inform the dis-
cussion on the political usage of data driven corporate regulation and enrich the
literature on transnational law as a political project16 as well as a method to
assess the political character of the private.
    The second chapter explores the link between the CSCS (thus the
elements of the SCS addressed to corporations) and the ‘transnational’,
including the conception of transnational law as a political project, thus as
a subject of analysis, but also as a method for analysis of a transnational
legal problem, such as the CSCS. In the third chapter, the transnational
nature of some elements of the CSCS, and the system’s possible transnational
impact, as well as the extraterritorial application of the CSCS, will be ana-
lysed through the lens of certain concepts of transnational law.
    The article hence seeks to answer the following questions:
(1) What is the relationship between the CSCS and transnational law as pol-
    itical project and what could transnational law as method contribute to
    the discussion of the CSCS?
(2) What is the potential transnational impact of CSCS on corporate behav-
    iour and the transnational construction and ﬂow of norms?
